children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: An individual ocean common carrier or an agreement between or among ocean common carriers may enter into a service contract with one or more shippers subject to the requirements of this part.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Each service contract entered into under this section by an individual ocean common carrier or an agreement shall be filed confidentially with the Federal Maritime Commission.

children.heading: .—Exceptions
children.label: 2 .—Exceptions
children.text: Paragraph (1) does not apply to contracts regarding bulk cargo, forest products, recycled metal scrap, new assembled motor vehicles, waste paper, or paper waste.
the origin and destination port ranges;
the origin and destination geographic areas in the case of through intermodal movements;
the commodities involved;
the minimum volume or portion;
the line-haul rate;
the duration;
service commitments;
the liquidated damages for nonperformance, if any; and
any other essential terms that the Federal Maritime Commission determines necessary or appropriate through a rulemaking process.

children.heading: .—Publication of Certain Terms
children.label: d .—Publication of Certain Terms
children.text: When a service contract is filed confidentially with the Commission, a concise statement of the essential terms specified in paragraphs (1), (3), (4), and (6) of subsection (c) shall be published and made available to the general public in tariff format.

children.heading: .—Definitions
children.label: 1 .—Definitions
children.text: In this subsection, the terms “dock area” and “within the port area” have the same meaning and scope as in the applicable collective bargaining agreement between the requesting labor organization and the carrier.

children.label: A
children.text: The movement of the shipper’s cargo on a dock area or within the port area or to or from railroad cars on a dock area or within the port area.

children.label: B
children.text: The assignment of intraport carriage of the shipper’s cargo between areas on a dock or within the port area.

children.label: C
children.text: The assignment of the carriage of the shipper’s cargo between a container yard on a dock area or within the port area and a rail yard adjacent to the container yard.

children.label: D
children.text: The assignment of container freight station work and container maintenance and repair work performed at a dock area or within the port area.

children.heading: .—Within reasonable time
children.label: 3 .—Within reasonable time
children.text: The common carrier shall provide the information described in paragraph (2) to the requesting labor organization within a reasonable period of time.

children.heading: .—Existence of collective bargaining agreement
children.label: 4 .—Existence of collective bargaining agreement
children.text: This subsection does not require the disclosure of information by an ocean common carrier unless there exists an applicable and otherwise lawful collective bargaining agreement pertaining to that carrier. A disclosure by an ocean common carrier may not be deemed an admission or an agreement that any work is covered by a collective bargaining agreement. A dispute about whether any work is covered by a collective bargaining agreement and the responsibility of an ocean common carrier under a collective bargaining agreement shall be resolved solely in accordance with the dispute resolution procedures contained in the collective bargaining agreement and the National Labor Relations Act ( et seq.), and without reference to this subsection.29 U.S.C. 151

children.heading: .—Effect under other laws
children.label: 5 .—Effect under other laws
children.text: This subsection does not affect the lawfulness or unlawfulness under this part or any other Federal or State law of any collective bargaining agreement or element thereof, including any element that constitutes an essential term of a service contract.

children.heading: .—Remedy for Breach
children.label: f .—Remedy for Breach
children.text: Unless the parties agree otherwise, the exclusive remedy for a breach of a service contract is an action in an appropriate court. The contract dispute resolution forum may not be controlled by or in any way affiliated with a controlled carrier or by the government that owns or controls the carrier.
